evidence_quotes.quote: sunset plan for games to allow keeping them alive in any form, like with private servers
The games are just gone, no files provided to allow people to host it themselves like fanmade servers, or no offline mode as it was always online game.
We stated that we may cut service in an unspecified time in the future.

paragraphs.text: Stop Killing Games, is the only fair way to avoid exploitation of consumers by big corporates that create problems to sell the Solution to or just run away with our money with no consequnces as by current Law, they are in the right for just cutting the service. I am no Lawyer and can not accurately word it in a way where its hole proof, but a sunset plan for games to allow keeping them alive in any form, like with private servers or them simply designed in a way that allows such would protect consumers from some very dirty business practices. As far as I am aware, currenlty there is nothing that in theory stops a company from realising a game, selling it to people and as a hyperbole close it after 2 weeks once they sold enough under the pretense of "We stated that we may cut service in an unspecified time in the future.", that would obviously be a bit of a huge scam, haven't heard of anyone actually pulling it off to that extreme, but we had some games that just died within 2 months. And the games are just gone, no files provided to allow people to host it themselves like fanmade servers, or no offline mode as it was always online game. Of course its also our duty as a Civilization as a whole, to archive and store as much of our Art, literature, data and I think games as well, as its part of our History and an expression of Art. SKG is also very fair to the Producers, putting no additional strain on them for past products, just implores them to keep in mind in future projects so that such business practices are less destructive to both the art and the Consumers purchase. Any claims that they do not want that to protect us, are just false. The internet has always been a place where users where at risk, claiming that private servers run be random people are providing a risk are maybe not entirely incorrect, if you aim for the low hanging fruit... But so is visiting every page on the internet ever, and its my risk to take.
